content.text: Discussions on adding an in-world admin settings menu to allow users to swap environment assets, assign builder roles, change default avatars, and export world folders.
Proposal for a Hyperfy-Link system to sync local development changes with a running Hyperfy world, requiring authentication for security.
Request for script-only apps to allow UI elements, lights, and skybox controls without requiring a GLB model.
Discussion on integrating AI to assist with coding and world-building, making Hyperfy more accessible to non-developers.
Concerns raised about the lack of clear documentation and onboarding for new users, leading to confusion about Hyperfy’s purpose and usage.
Debate on monetization strategies for Hyperfy creators, including in-world transactions, asset sales, and a potential creator fund.
Proposal for a multiplayer zombie survival game using Hyperfy’s physics and scripting capabilities, with monetization through skins and upgrades.
Idea to consolidate all in-game menus into a single, gamepad-friendly interface for improved usability.
Work on a modular control panel (MCP) server for managing Hyperfy apps, with ongoing improvements to performance and authentication.
Implementation of a three-click power meter UI mechanic for controlling power and accuracy in sports-based mini-games.
Community discussions on Hyperfy’s competition, including Hytopia, which is building a Minecraft-like experience with a Roblox spin.
Suggestion for Hyperfy to host a contest similar to levelsio’s, with potential rewards for creators.
Marketing suggestion to reach out to web3 gaming influencers like Alex Becker for exposure.
Discussion on the Dungeons Game funding model, where users deposit funds, and the price grows to a cap, with additional deposits adjusting stake.

### Admin Settings & World Customization
A proposal was discussed to add an in-world admin settings menu, allowing users to swap environment assets, assign builder roles, change default avatars, and export world folders.

### Hyperfy-Link for Local Development
A system was proposed where a local Hyperfy world could detect a running local development server and sync code changes via a 'link' button in the editor. Authentication would be required to prevent unauthorized access.

### AI-Powered Development
Discussions took place on integrating AI to assist with coding and world-building, making Hyperfy more accessible to non-developers.

### Documentation & Onboarding Issues
Concerns were raised about the lack of clear documentation and onboarding for new users, leading to confusion about what Hyperfy is and how to use it.

### Hosting & Monetization
A debate took place on how creators can monetize their worlds, with suggestions including in-world transactions, asset sales, and a potential creator fund to support high-quality projects.

### Contest Idea
A suggestion was made to host a Hyperfy contest similar to one by levelsio. The idea was acknowledged as something previously discussed.

### Marketing Suggestion
A proposal was made to reach out to Alex Becker and his web3 gaming guild for exposure. The concept of a web3 gaming guild was explained as a group of developers, players, and investors in crypto gaming.

### Water Texture Scrolling
A code snippet was shared for implementing scrolling diffuse and normal textures for water using `app.on('update')`.

### Texture Memory Concern
A discussion took place regarding whether 24MB was excessive for three textures. It was explained that textures are decompressed and uploaded to the GPU in a raw format, making them larger than their compressed file size.

### Animation Library Discussion
A link to Quaternius' Universal Animation Library was shared. Concerns were raised about VRM compatibility due to custom bone naming.

### AI-Generated Facades
AI-generated facades were discussed, with a focus on color-matching them to the skybox for improved immersion.

### Current SOL Price
The current price of SOL was reported as $125.33.
